message: Got the notes from yesterday. Corn-to-batter ratio was unstable - heat management was fine but the structure collapsed by hour three. We're starting over on that part.

The collapse makes sense - that much cornmeal wants a binder that actually holds, which changes what we're asking the recipe to *be*. What if we lean into that brittleness instead of fighting it, make the bite-through the whole story?
That's not brittleness, that's failure. We need cornmeal to stay suspended - more egg, less flour, test the gluten development.
You're right - I was romanticizing the problem instead of solving it. More egg and less flour actually makes sense for what cornmeal needs structurally.
I like how you caught yourself there - that's the kind of thinking that gets us to the real answer, not the pretty one.
I think the ratios work - want to pull test batches tomorrow morning and see if the crumb stays tight?
